% Auto-generated by sees_run.py. Do not edit by hand.
% Known-truth DGP cells: canonical_low_action, canonical_high_action

\section{Generated Validation Results}
This section is generated by \texttt{sees\_run.py} from the \texttt{experiments.known\_truth} harness. Hard gates are enforced. The low-dimensional cell is a sanity check for the historical index-based sieve; the high-dimensional cell is the main SEES validation because it uses the encoded-state sieve.

\subsection{Validation Design}
Both cells use 2,000 simulated individuals, 80 periods per individual, known transitions, three actions, homogeneous rewards, and the shared Type A/B/C counterfactual oracles.

\begin{table}[H]
\centering\scriptsize
\caption{SEES known-truth validation cells.}
\begin{tabular}{@{}llrrrrrr@{}}
\toprule
Cell & Role & States & State dim. & Reward params & Basis & Penalty & Gates \\
\midrule
Low-dimensional & sanity & 21 & 2 & 4 & state\_index, K=21 & 100 & 11/11 \\
High-dimensional & primary & 81 & 16 & 32 & encoded\_state, K=81 & 10000 & 11/11 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

The high-dimensional DGP passes pre-estimation checks before SEES is fit: feature rank is 32/32, the reward-feature condition number is 1.377, all 81 states are observed, and state-action coverage is 0.959. The encoded-state basis uses 16 state features and has numerical rank 81.

\subsection{Fit Summary}
\begin{table}[H]
\centering\small
\caption{SEES fit summary.}
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrr}
\toprule
Cell & Optimizer flag & Iter. & Bellman max & Bellman RMSE & Log likelihood \\
\midrule
\texttt{canonical\_low\_action} & false & 181 & $5.83\times 10^{-5}$ & $2.74\times 10^{-5}$ & -174875.8 \\
\texttt{canonical\_high\_action} & false & 6 & $3.08\times 10^{-6}$ & $6.80\times 10^{-7}$ & -160289.8 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

The optimizer flag is reported exactly as returned by L-BFGS-B. The hard SEES validation gate is the Bellman residual, finite standard errors, known-truth recovery, and counterfactual regret.

\subsection{Main Recovery Numbers}
\begin{table}[H]
\centering\small
\caption{Core recovery metrics.}
\begin{tabular}{llrr}
\toprule
Metric & Gate & Low-dimensional & High-dimensional \\
\midrule
Bellman violation & $\leq 0.05$ & 0.000058 & 0.000003 \\
Parameter cosine similarity & $\geq 0.99$ & 0.999146 & 0.999955 \\
Parameter relative RMSE & $\leq 0.15$ & 0.059671 & 0.009528 \\
Reward RMSE & $\leq 0.03$ & 0.008988 & 0.004432 \\
Policy total variation & $\leq 0.02$ & 0.005179 & 0.002117 \\
Value RMSE & $\leq 0.10$ & 0.017591 & 0.037836 \\
Q RMSE & $\leq 0.10$ & 0.020514 & 0.031480 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection{Counterfactual Recovery}
Each counterfactual gate requires regret below 0.01.

\begin{table}[H]
\centering\small
\caption{Counterfactual regret.}
\begin{tabular}{lrrl}
\toprule
Counterfactual & Low-dimensional & High-dimensional & Interpretation \\
\midrule
Type A & 0.000179 & 0.000113 & reward/state shift \\
Type B & 0.000299 & 0.000183 & transition change \\
Type C & 0.000071 & 0.000014 & action restriction \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

Exact parameter vectors, standard errors, log-likelihoods, policy distances, basis metadata, and all gate values are written to \texttt{sees\_results.json}. The PDF reports the decision-critical values rather than printing the full 32-parameter high-dimensional vector.
